description:
Add Elixir-style `@spec` and `@type` declarations. Declared specs are an **upper bound** on the typer's inferred specs (option 1 / success-typing semantics): validation passes iff `inferred ⊆ declared`. Narrower inferred is fine (user is hiding info deliberately); disjoint or wider is an error.

Builds on:
- fz-ul4.18.6 (closed): @doc/@moduledoc, generic @name parser scaffold, FnDef.doc field
- fz-ul4.29 epic: per-callsite typer specialization producing ModuleTypes.specs
- fz-ul4.20.5/.20.8: diagnostic taxonomy + Descr Display

## Children DAG

.31.1 ──┬─► .31.2 ──┬─► .31.3 ──┐
        │           │            ├─► .31.4 ──► .31.5 ──► .31.6
        │           └────────────┘
        └─► .31.3

(fz-ul4.29.10 also blocks .31.5)

- .31.1: Type-expression parser → Descr (pure)
- .31.2: Generalize FnDef/ModuleDef attribute representation
- .31.3: @type aliases + module type environment
- .31.4: @spec parser + AST attachment
- .31.5: Validation pass (inferred ⊆ declared) — depends on .29.10
- .31.6: REPL ?<name> + three-path parity golden test

## Out of scope (explicitly deferred — separate epics)

- @callback / @impl (protocols/behaviours)
- @opaque types
- Parameterized type aliases: @type result(t) :: {:ok, t} | :error
- Persisting declared specs in the module artifact for cross-module checking
- Declared specs narrowing codegen specialization (option 3 — we chose option 1: declared is check only, not codegen input)
title: [EPIC] @spec / @type declarations
